notes: Confirmed: NativeAssets.WebAssembly MSBuild targets only activate for Blazor (UsingMicrosoftNETSdkBlazorWebAssembly/UsingMicrosoftNETSdkWebAssembly) and Uno Platform (IsUnoHead + UnoRuntimeIdentifier=WebAssembly) apps. WASI console apps using wasi-wasm RID with Microsoft.NET.Sdk set none of these properties, so NativeFileReference items are never populated and the .a static libraries are never linked. Build succeeds but output contains only managed SkiaSharp.dll — no native libSkiaSharp. DllNotFoundException at runtime is the expected consequence. Gap persists across all tested versions including main branch source.

reproductionSteps.command: dotnet new console -n Repro --framework net9.0 && edit csproj to add wasi-wasm RID + SkiaSharp packages

reproductionSteps.description: Check MSBuild properties — verify Blazor/Uno SDK conditions are not set for WASI
Check NativeFileReference MSBuild items — verify .a files are not linked for WASI projects
Build project — succeeds but output has no native libSkiaSharp
reproductionSteps.output: Build succeeded. Output: Repro.dll, Repro.pdb, Repro.deps.json, Repro.runtimeconfig.json, SkiaSharp.dll (managed only). No libSkiaSharp.a in output.

reproductionSteps.description: Inspect NuGet package targets — only Blazor and Uno conditions present, no WASI support
reproductionSteps.output: Targets contain two condition groups: (1) IsUnoHead=True AND UnoRuntimeIdentifier=WebAssembly, (2) UsingMicrosoftNETSdkBlazorWebAssembly=true. No conditions for WASI, wasi-wasm RID, or generic WebAssembly projects.

reproductionSteps.description: Repeat with latest stable (3.119.2) — same MSBuild gap, NativeFileReference empty
reproductionSteps.command: Create fresh project dir with 3.119.2 packages, dotnet msbuild -getItem:NativeFileReference
reproductionSteps.output: NativeFileReference: []. 3.119.2 targets add UsingMicrosoftNETSdkWebAssembly as additional condition and net9.0+ support, but still no WASI conditions. Same gap.

reproductionSteps.description: Investigate main branch source — search for WASI support in NativeAssets.WebAssembly and native build scripts
reproductionSteps.output: No WASI references found in any SkiaSharp source files. Main branch buildTransitive/SkiaSharp.targets is identical to 3.119.2 NuGet package targets.

versionResults.notes: MSBuild targets only check UsingMicrosoftNETSdkBlazorWebAssembly and IsUnoHead — no WASI conditions. NativeFileReference empty for wasi-wasm RID.
Adds UsingMicrosoftNETSdkWebAssembly condition and net9.0+ support, but still no WASI conditions. NativeFileReference empty.
Main branch source targets identical to 3.119.2 NuGet package. No WASI references found anywhere in the codebase.

actionability.reason: Valid feature request. WASI Wasm is a distinct platform from browser WASM. The MSBuild targets were designed for Blazor/Uno only. Adding WASI support requires both MSBuild target changes and investigation into whether Emscripten-compiled .a files are compatible with the WASI toolchain.

actions.description: Reclassify as feature-request and add WASM platform label

proposedResponse.body:
Confirmed that `SkiaSharp.NativeAssets.WebAssembly` does not support WASI Wasm projects.

**Root cause:** The NuGet package's MSBuild targets only activate for:
- **Blazor** apps (`UsingMicrosoftNETSdkBlazorWebAssembly` or `UsingMicrosoftNETSdkWebAssembly`)
- **Uno Platform** apps (`IsUnoHead` + `UnoRuntimeIdentifier=WebAssembly`)

WASI console apps use the plain `Microsoft.NET.Sdk` with `wasi-wasm` RID, which sets none of these properties. The `.a` static libraries are present in the package but `NativeFileReference` items are never populated, so the native library is never linked into the output binary — causing the `DllNotFoundException` at runtime.

Tested on versions 3.0.0-preview.4.1, 3.119.2, and main branch source — the gap exists in all versions. No WASI-specific support exists in the codebase.

Note: Beyond the MSBuild gap, it's unclear whether the Emscripten-compiled static libraries are compatible with the WASI toolchain (wasmtime), or whether a separate WASI-targeted native build would be required.

missingInfo: Whether Emscripten-compiled .a files are compatible with WASI SDK/toolchain
Whether .NET WASI support is stable enough for official SkiaSharp support
Whether NativeFileReference static linking mechanism works identically for WASI projects
